headline: Juvenile detention facilities lack education for 670 minors
context: The Prisons Department reported that 670 juveniles in detention facilities had limited or no access to formal education programmes in 2024.

cards.big: 670 detained juveniles lack formal education access in Malaysian facilities
cards.sub: Child Act 2001 mandates education but implementation falls short

cards.big: Child Act 2001 Section 91 requires provision of education in approved schools
cards.sub: Only 3 of 8 juvenile facilities have certified teachers on staff

cards.big: CRC Article 28 guarantees detained children's right to continue education
cards.sub: Malaysia's juvenile justice system fails to meet international minimum standards

cards.big: Recidivism among juveniles without education access is 43% within 2 years
cards.sub: Education during detention is strongest predictor of successful reintegration

cards.big: Denying education to 670 detained children creates future offenders, not reformed youth
Education Ministry assigned 12 teachers to juvenile facilities in pilot program for 2025
